zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Camville於大約1178年出生在英國奧可福爾斯特拉頓 奧德利時，他的父親Gerard de Camville36歲，母親Nicola de la Haye28歲。他於大約1203年與Eustachia De Basset在英國英格蘭奧可福爾結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。他去世於1226年在英國享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Camville于大约1178年出生在英国奧可福爾斯特拉頓 奧德利时，他的父亲Gerard de Camville36岁，母亲Nicola de la Haye28岁。他于大约1203年与Eustachia De Basset在英国英格兰奧可福爾结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。他去世于1226年在英国享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Camville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French; English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the places in Calvados and Seine-Maritime named Carville, from the Scandinavian personal name Kári + Old French ville ‘settlement’.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Cearbhaill or Mac Cearbhaill, see Carroll .

variants.name: Carroll
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Cearbhaill or Mac Cearbhaill ‘descendant (or son) of Cearbhall’, a personal name perhaps based on cearbh ‘hacking’ and hence originally a byname for a butcher or a fierce warrior.
English and Scottish: variant of   Carrell  .

variants.name: Garfield
meanings: English (Midlands): topographic name from Middle English gor(e), gar(e) ‘triangular piece’ + feld ‘open country, field’ (Old English gār + feld), for someone who lived by a triangular piece of land.
English: alternatively, sometimes perhaps a variant of Carville (a name of Norman origin), with excrescent final -d.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Camville was born about 1178, in Stratton Audley, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Gerard de Camville, was 36 and his mother, Nicola de la Haye, was 28. He married Eustachia De Basset about 1203, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. He died in 1226, in England, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Camville est né environ 1178, à Stratton Audley, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Gerard de Camville, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Nicola de la Haye, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Eustachia De Basset environ 1203, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1226, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Camville um 1178 in Stratton Audley, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gerard de Camville 36 und seine Mutter Nicola de la Haye 28. Er hat um 1203 in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom Eustachia De Basset geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. Er starb 1226 im Alter von 49 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Camville è nato il circa 1178 nel Stratton Audley, Oxfordshire, England, il suo padre, Gerard de Camville, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Nicola de la Haye, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Eustachia De Basset circa 1203, nel Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figlie. Morì nel 1226, nel England, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Camvilleが約1178年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアストラットオン アウドリーで生まれた時，父Gerard de Camvilleは36歳，母Nicola de la Hayeは28歳であった。約1203年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアでEustachia De Bassetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。彼は1226年にイギリスで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Camville(이)가 대략 1178년 영국 스퍼드실르 스트래턴 오들리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerard de Camville(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Nicola de la Haye(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1203년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 Eustachia De Basset(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1226년 영국에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Creeke, Northamptonshire, England: North Hamtunscire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Camville nasceu aproximadamente 1178, em Stratton Audley, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Gerard de Camville, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Nicola de la Haye, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Eustachia De Basset aproximadamente 1203, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1226, em Inglaterra, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Camville родился около 1178 г. в местности страттон аудли, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Gerard de Camville было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Nicola de la Haye, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Eustachia De Basset около 1203 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Он умер в 1226 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 49 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Camville nació aproximadamente 1178, en Stratton Audley, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Gerard de Camville, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Nicola de la Haye, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Eustachia De Basset aproximadamente 1203, en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas. Murió en 1226, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 49 años.
